{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks for the benzodiazepine antagonist. Step 1: Identify the correct pharmacological agent that specifically antagonizes benzodiazepines. Furazolidone (B) is not mentioned in any context related to benzodiazepines; it is an antibiotic. Naloxone (C) and naltrexone (D) are opioid antagonists, not benzodiazepine antagonists. The UMLS documents confirm that naltrexone and naloxone are used for opioid dependence and overdose, not benzodiazepine-related conditions. Therefore, only flumazenil fits the definition of a benzodiazepine antagonist.
